input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI targeting Android + Windows on Windows 11:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Discover latest .NET SDK versions
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# Install (e.g., .NET 9)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --silent

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Java JDK (Android build requirement)

```powershell
# MAUI installs Microsoft OpenJDK automatically via workload,
# but to confirm or install manually:
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK

# Install JDK 17 (required by Android Gradle)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --silent

# Verify
java --version
```

## 4. Install Android SDK (if not bundled sufficiently)

```powershell
# The MAUI workload auto-provisions Android SDK components.
# Check what was installed:
dotnet build -t:ResolveAndroidTooling -v:n

# If you need the standalone SDK/cmdline tools:
winget install Google.AndroidCommandLineTools --silent

# Accept licenses
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Discover & Install Required Android API Levels

```powershell
# List available platforms dynamically
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
    Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install target platform (e.g., API 35) and build tools
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "platforms;android-35" `
    "build-tools;35.0.0" `
    "platform-tools"
```

## 6. Enable Windows App SDK Target (Developer Mode)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows target)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f

# Verify
Get-ItemProperty "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock"
```

## 7. Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Run on Android Emulator

```powershell
# Create an AVD (discover system images first)
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
    Select-String "system-images.*google_apis;x86_64"

# Install a system image
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create emulator
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" `
    create avd -n "Pixel_API35" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --force

# Launch emulator (async)
Start-Process "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\emulator\emulator.exe" `
    -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_API35"

# Deploy
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install
```

## 9. (Optional) Install Visual Studio for GUI Tooling

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --silent `
    --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat --passive"
```

---

**Key tips:**
- Replace `net9.0` with whatever `dotnet --list-sdks` shows as your major version.
- The MAUI workload handles most Android SDK provisioning; steps 4–5 are only needed if builds complain about missing components.
- Restart your terminal after installs so `PATH` updates take effect.
